sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 140, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 139 actions; a strong score needs about 28 or fewer. You are 5.0x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key findings from transitions 10-30:
- Transition 18: UP caused a SWAP! Before: greens at (30-32, 27-29) and (33-35, 12-14). After: greens at (30-32, 12-14) and (30-32, 27-29). So UP when one green is at row 30-32 and the other at row 33-35 causes a SWAP - the bottom green moves UP to the top green's position, and the top green moves DOWN to the bottom green's position.
- Transition 23: RIGHT moved the left green from (30-32, 21-23) to (30-32, 24-25) with 5 pixels - the green box got damaged (changed from 8 to 5 pixels)
- Transition 24: RIGHT moved the left green from (30-32, 24-25) to (30-32, 24-26) with 8 pixels, AND the right green moved from (30-32, 27-29) to (30-32, 42-44) with 8 pixels! This is a TELEPORT! The right green jumped from the left panel to the right panel!
So the key mechanic is: when a green box is damaged (5 pixels) and moved RIGHT, it can teleport the other green box to the right panel!
Let me trace the sequence:
1. Both greens at row 30-32: left at (30-32, 12-14), right at (30-32, 27-29)
2. RIGHT x3: left green moves to (30-32, 21-23)
3. RIGHT x1: left green moves to (30-32, 24-25) with 5 pixels (damaged)
4. RIGHT x1: left green moves to (30-32, 24-26) with 8 pixels (repaired), and right green teleports to (30-32, 42-44)!
So the sequence is: when both greens are at the same row, move the left green RIGHT until it gets damaged (5 pixels), then RIGHT one more time to repair it and teleport the right green to the right panel.
After the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44) in the right panel. The right charcoal box interior is at (27-29, 45-47). So I need to move the right green UP to row 27-29 and RIGHT to col 45-47.
But wait, after the teleport, both greens are at row 30-32. The left green is at (30-32, 24-26) and the right green is at (30-32, 42-44). The left green is the leftmost, so UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the left green.
To move the right green, I need to make it the leftmost. I can do this by moving the left green to col > 44, or by using DOWN to swap them.
Actually, looking at transition 24, after the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44). The right charcoal box interior is at (27-29, 45-47). So I need to move the right green UP 3 rows and RIGHT 3 columns.
But the right green is in the right panel, and the left green is in the left panel. The purple bar is between them. So I can't move the left green to the right of the right green.
To move the right green, I need to make it the leftmost. One way is to use DOWN to swap the greens. But the swap requires both greens to be at the same row, which they are (row 30-32).
Wait, but the swap mechanic from transition 6 was: DOWN when both greens are at the same row causes the left green to move to the right green's position, and the right green to move DOWN 3 rows. So after DOWN, the left green would be at (30-32, 42-44) and the right green would be at (33-35, 24-26). Then the right green would be the leftmost (col 24-26 < 42-44), so I can move it.
But wait, I need the right green to be at (27-29, 45-47), not at (33-35, 24-26). So the swap doesn't help directly.
Let me think about this differently. After the teleport (transition 24), the state is:
- Left green: (30-32, 24-26), 8 pixels
- Right green: (30-32, 42-44), 8 pixels
Both at row 30-32. The left green is the leftmost.
If I move the left green RIGHT, it goes to (30-32, 27-29). Then RIGHT again, it goes to (30-32, 30-32). Then RIGHT again, it goes to... where? The purple bar is at cols 33-38, so the left green can't move past col 32.
Hmm, but the bridge is at rows 27-29, cols 27-32. So if I move the left green UP to row 27-29, it can move RIGHT through the bridge.
Wait, but the left green is at row 30-32, and the bridge is at row 27-29. If I move the left green UP, it goes to row 27-29. Then RIGHT moves it through the bridge to the right panel.
But the right green is also at row 30-32. If I move the left green UP, it goes to row 27-29, and the right green stays at row 30-32. Then the left green is the leftmost (col 24-26 < 42-44), so I can continue moving it RIGHT through the bridge.
But wait, the left green at (27-29, 24-26) can move RIGHT to (27-29, 27-29), then (27-29, 30-32), then... the bridge ends at col 32, and the purple bar starts at col 33. So the left green can't move further RIGHT.
Actually, looking at the ASCII, the bridge is at rows 27-29, cols 17-25 (gray area). Let me re-examine.
Row 26: `gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgggggggggppppppwwwwwcccccwwwwwgg`
Row 27: `gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgggggggggppppppwwwwwcwwwcwwwwwgg`
The gray area (g) is at cols 17-25, and the purple area (p) is at cols 26-31. So the bridge is at cols 17-25, not 27-32.
Wait, but the greens are at cols 12-14, 15-17, 18-20, 21-23, 24-26, 27-29, etc. So the green at col 24-26 is partially in the gray area (cols 17-25).
Actually, I'm getting confused with the column indices. Let me just focus on the game mechanics.
From transition 24, I know that RIGHT can teleport the right green to the right panel. After the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44). The right charcoal box interior is at (27-29, 45-47).
To move the right green to (27-29, 45-47), I need to:
1. Make the right green the leftmost (so UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves it)
2. Move it UP to row 27-29
3. Move it RIGHT to col 45-47
To make the right green the leftmost, I can use DOWN to swap the greens. After DOWN, the left green moves to the right green's position (30-32, 42-44), and the right green moves DOWN to (33-35, 24-26). Then the right green is the leftmost (col 24-26 < 42-44), so I can move it.
But wait, after the swap, the right green is at (33-35, 24-26), which is in the left panel. I need it to be at (27-29, 45-47), which is in the right panel. So the swap doesn't help.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. After the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44) in the right panel. The right charcoal box is at (26-30, 44-48), with interior at (27-29, 45-47).
If I can move the right green UP to row 27-29 and RIGHT to col 45-47, it would be inside the right charcoal box.
But to move the right green, I need to make it the leftmost. Since the right green is at col 42-44 and the left green is at col 24-26, the left green is the leftmost.
One idea: move the left green RIGHT until it's at col > 44, making the right green the leftmost. But the left green is in the left panel, and the purple bar blocks movement to the right panel. So the left green can't reach col > 44.
Another idea: use DOWN to swap the greens. After DOWN, the left green is at (30-32, 42-44) and the right green is at (33-35, 24-26). Then the right green is the leftmost, so I can move it. But the right green is now in the left panel, not the right panel.
Wait, but after the swap, the left green is in the right panel at (30-32, 42-44). I can move the left green (which is now in the right panel) to the right charcoal box interior at (27-29, 45-47). Then the goal might be achieved.
Let me trace this:
1. After teleport: left green at (30-32, 24-26), right green at (30-32, 42-44)
2. DOWN: swap - left green moves to (30-32, 42-44), right green moves to (33-35, 24-26)
3. Now left green is at (30-32, 42-44) in the right panel, right green is at (33-35, 24-26) in the left panel
4. The right green is the leftmost (col 24-26 < 42-44), so UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the right green
5. To move the left green (which is in the right panel), I need to make it the leftmost
6. Move the right green RIGHT to col > 44, making the left green the leftmost
7. Then move the left green UP to row 27-29 and RIGHT to col 45-47
But the right green is in the left panel, and moving it RIGHT might not get it past col 44.
Actually, let me re-examine the swap mechanic. From transition 6:
Before: left green at (30-32, 18-20), right green at (30-32, 27-29)
After DOWN: left green at (30-32, 27-29), right green at (33-35, 18-20)
So the left green moves to the right green's position, and the right green moves DOWN 3 rows to the left green's original column.
After the teleport (transition 24):
- Left green at (30-32, 24-26)
- Right green at (30-32, 42-44)
After DOWN:
- Left green moves to (30-32, 42-44)
- Right green moves to (33-35, 24-26)
Now the right green is at (33-35, 24-26), which is the leftmost. So UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the right green.
To move the left green (at 30-32, 42-44), I need to make it the leftmost. I can do this by moving the right green to col > 44. But the right green is in the left panel, and the purple bar blocks movement to the right panel.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me try a different approach.
Actually, wait. Looking at transition 24 more carefully:
Before: left green at (30-32, 24-25) with 5 pixels, right green at (30-32, 27-29) with 8 pixels
After: left green at (30-32, 24-26) with 8 pixels, right green at (30-32, 42-44) with 8 pixels
So the teleport happens when the left green is damaged (5 pixels) and moved RIGHT. The left green gets repaired (8 pixels), and the right green teleports to the right panel.
Now, after the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44). The right charcoal box interior is at (27-29, 45-47). So I need to move the right green UP 3 rows and RIGHT 3 columns.
But the right green is at col 42-44, and the left green is at col 24-26. The left green is the leftmost, so UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the left green.
What if I move the left green UP to row 27-29, then RIGHT through the bridge to the right panel? Then the left green would be in the right panel, and I could move it to the right charcoal box.
But the bridge is at rows 27-29, and the left green is at row 30-32. If I move the left green UP, it goes to row 27-29. Then RIGHT moves it... where? The bridge connects the left panel to the right panel at rows 27-29.
Actually, looking at the ASCII:
Row 27: `gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgggggggggppppppwwwwwcwwwcwwwwwgg`
The gray area (g) is at cols 17-25, and the purple area (p) is at cols 26-31. So the bridge is at cols 17-25, not connecting to the right panel.
Wait, but the right panel is at cols 32+ (white area). The purple bar is at cols 26-31. So the bridge (gray area) doesn't connect to the right panel; it's just a gray area in the left panel.
Hmm, but the teleport mechanic moves the right green from the left panel to the right panel. So there must be a way to move greens between panels.
Let me look at the ASCII more carefully. The right panel has the right charcoal box at rows 26-30, cols 37-41 (based on the ASCII). Wait, the segmentation says the right charcoal box is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. There's a discrepancy.
Actually, I think the ASCII columns don't match the segmentation columns because the ASCII string might have a different format or offset. Let me just trust the segmentation.
From the segmentation:
- Right charcoal box: boundary=[[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 44]], pixels=16
- Interior: rows 27-29, cols 45-47
- Right green after teleport: (30-32, 42-44)
So the right green is at row 30-32, col 42-44. The right charcoal box interior is at row 27-29, col 45-47.
To move the right green to the interior, I need UP x1 (from row 30-32 to row 27-29) and RIGHT x1 (from col 42-44 to col 45-47).
But the right green is not the leftmost, so UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the left green.
OK so I need to get back to the initial state and try a more direct approach. Let me figure out the exact sequence from the initial state:
1. Initial: left green at (30-32, 18-20), right green at (30-32, 27-29)
2. RIGHT x3: left green to (30-32, 21-23)
3. RIGHT x1: left green to (30-32, 24-25) with 5 pixels (damaged)
4. RIGHT x1: left green to (30-32, 24-26) with 8 pixels (repaired), right green teleports to (30-32, 42-44)
5. DOWN: swap - left green to (30-32, 42-44), right green to (33-35, 24-26)
6. Now right green is leftmost (col 24-26 < 42-44)
7. Move right green RIGHT x... to col > 44, making left green the leftmost
8. Then move left green UP and RIGHT to (27-29, 45-47)
But step 7 is problematic because the right green is in the left panel and can't move past the purple bar.
Wait, maybe I don't need to swap. After step 4, the state is:
- Left green: (30-32, 24-26), 8 pixels
- Right green: (30-32, 42-44), 8 pixels
Both at row 30-32. The left green is the leftmost.
What if I move the left green RIGHT x2 to (30-32, 30-32)? Then the left green is at the edge of the left panel. Then RIGHT x1 more... what happens? Does the left green teleport to the right panel?
Actually, looking at transition 24, the teleport happened when the left green was damaged (5 pixels). After repair, the right green teleported. So maybe the teleport is a one-time thing that only happens when a green is damaged and repaired.
Let me try a different approach. After the teleport, the right green is at (30-32, 42-44). What if I use DOWN to swap, then move the right green (now at 33-35, 24-26) UP to row 30-32, matching the left green's row? Then both greens are at different rows, and I can move the right green independently.
Actually, I realize I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just try to execute the sequence from the initial state and see what happens.
But I'm not at the initial state. I need to reset first. Let me check if there's a way to reset the state.
Actually, looking at the early transitions, the initial state was:
- Left green: (30-32, 18-20), 8 pixels
- Right green: (30-32, 27-29), 8 pixels
Current state:
- Left green: (33-34, 21-23), 5 pixels
- Right green: (36-38, 21-23), 8 pixels
I need to get back to the initial state. One way is to use UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT to move the greens back.
But the current state is quite different from the initial state. The left green is damaged (5 pixels), and both greens are at the same column.
Let me try to repair the left green by moving it LEFT or RIGHT. From earlier observations, moving a green box through a charcoal box repairs it.
The left charcoal box is at (32-36, 11-15), with interior at (33-35, 12-14). The left green is at (33-34, 21-23). If I move the left green LEFT x3, it goes to (33-34, 12-14), which is inside the left charcoal box. This might repair it.
Let me try LEFT x3 to move the left green to (33-34, 12-14).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Key findings from transitions:
# - DOWN when both greens at same row causes SWAP
# - RIGHT can damage a green (8->5 pixels) then repair it (5->8) and teleport the other green to right panel
# - After teleport, right green at (30-32, 42-44)
#
# Current state: left green at (33-34, 21-23) with 5 pixels, right green at (36-38, 21-23) with 8 pixels
# Both at same col 21-23, left green is topmost
#
# Need to get to initial state: left at (30-32, 18-20), right at (30-32, 27-29)
# First, repair left green by moving it into left charcoal box (33-35, 12-14)
# LEFT x3 should move left green from col 21-23 to col 12-14

result = action([{'action': 'LEFT'}, {'action': 'LEFT'}, {'action': 'LEFT'}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("After LEFT x3:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        r_min, c_min = n['boundary'][0]
        r_max, c_max = n['boundary'][2]
        center_r = (r_min + r_max) // 2
        center_c = (c_min + c_max) // 2
        dot_char = ascii_lines[center_r][center_c]
        print(f"  Green at ({r_min}-{r_max}, {c_min}-{c_max}), center=({center_r},{center_c})={dot_char}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
After LEFT x3:
  Green at (33-35, 12-14), center=(34,13)=W, pixels=8
  Green at (36-38, 21-23), center=(37,22)=c, pixels=8
Level completed: False

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Key findings from transitions:
# - DOWN when both greens at same row causes SWAP
# - RIGHT can damage a green (8->5 pixels) then repair it (5->8) and teleport the other green to right panel
# - After teleport, right green at (30-32, 42-44)
#
# Current state: left green at (33-34, 21-23) with 5 pixels, right green at (36-38, 21-23) with 8 pixels
# Both at same col 21-23, left green is topmost
#
# Need to get to initial state: left at (30-32, 18-20), right at (30-32, 27-29)
# First, repair left green by moving it into left charcoal box (33-35, 12-14)
# LEFT x3 should move left green from col 21-23 to col 12-14

result = action([{'action': 'LEFT'}, {'action': 'LEFT'}, {'action': 'LEFT'}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("After LEFT x3:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        r_min, c_min = n['boundary'][0]
        r_max, c_max = n['boundary'][2]
        center_r = (r_min + r_max) // 2
        center_c = (c_min + c_max) // 2
        dot_char = ascii_lines[center_r][center_c]
        print(f"  Green at ({r_min}-{r_max}, {c_min}-{c_max}), center=({center_r},{center_c})={dot_char}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
